title: mandevilla pohliana var. velutina
intro: Mandevilla pohliana var. velutina is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the velvet mandevilla, which is characterized by its soft, velvety leaves and trumpet-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its purported medicinal properties, such as antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The velvet mandevilla is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
